title: Connecting device for fiber optics
abstract: The current invention concerns a hermaphroditic device (1) for connecting axially aligned elements, suitable for connecting fiber optics, comprising a body (3), a axially aligned protruding external screw threaded cylinder (2) at one axial end and a sunken axially aligned annular cavity (4) at the other axial end.
claims:
Claims CLAI MS 1 . A herm aphroditic device for connecting axially aligned elem ents, suitable for connecting fiber optics, comprising a body, a axially aligned protruding external screw threaded cylinder at one axial end and a sunken axially aligned annular cavity at the other axial end. 2. A device according to claim 1 wherein the sunken axially aligned annular cavity comprises a ring cylindered sleeve form ing a pluggable port. 3. A device according to claim 1 or 2, wherein at least one surface of said device is magnetic, preferably wherein at least one outer surface of the body is flat and/or m agnetic. 4. A device according to any of the previous claims 1 -3, wherein said protruding end is solid integrated with said body. 5. A device according to any of the previous claims 2-4, wherein said body comprises an integrated thin wall sleeve matched with the inner surface of said ring cylindered sleeve. 6. A device according to any of the previous claims 1 -5, wherein the cross section orthogonal to the axial centreline of said body is a square. 7. A device according to, to any of the previous claims 1 -6, wherein said body is of a cubic shape. 8. A device according to, to any of the previous claims 1 -7 wherein said body com prises an inner and outer surface and wherein the outer surface of said body is m agnetic. 9. A device according to claim 8, wherein the opposite two outer surfaces are with opposite magnetic poles. 1 0. A device according to any of the previous claims 1 -9, wherein the inner surface of the pluggable port end is m agnetic. 1 1 . A device according to any of the previous claims 1 - 10, whereby two fiber optics are interconnected and whereby one fiber optic is connected with a m onitoring device for radiation data analysing and the other fiber optic is connected with an in-vivo dosimeter. 12. A plurality of device according to any of the previous claims 1 - 1 1 which are m agnetically grouped. 13. A kit for assem bling a m onitoring system for the matter of in-vivo dosimeter in radiotherapy, com prising : a monitoring device for receiving and analysing the radiation data from the end tips of fiber optics, a plurality of said connecting devices according to any of the claims 1 - 1 1 , a plurality of in-vivo 

 dosim eters applied in a subject, a plurality of pre-protected optical extensions linking said m onitoring device and said connecting device, a plurality of fiber optics linking the said connecting device and said dosimeter. 14. An assembly for the m atter of in-vivo dosimeter in radiotherapy, com prising: a m onitoring device for receiving and analysing radiation data from an end tip of a fiber optic, a connecting device according to any of the claims 1 - 1 1 , a dosimeter, a pre-protected optical extension linking said monitoring device and said connecting device and a fiber optic linking the connecting device and said dosim eter. 1 5. Use of the hermaphroditic device according to any of preceding claims 1 - 1 1 , the plurality of device according to preceding claim 12, the kit according to preceding claim 13 or the assem bly according to preceding claim 14, for radiotherapy, preferably for in vivo dosim etry during radiotherapy.
